---
pagetitle: "neuroim"
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
# neuroim

[Python data structures and algorithms for 3D, 4D, and 5D+ neuroimaging data.]{.lead}

[Get started](get-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Browse the API](reference/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

## Why neuroim?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Spatially aware

Volumes, vectors, and ROIs keep voxel data tied to `NeuroSpace`, so array operations preserve spatial metadata.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Memory conscious

Dense, sparse, mapped, and file-backed objects let the same workflows scale from examples to large imaging series.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Typed spatial workflows

Validated spaces, masks, ROI coordinates, and image objects make analysis code explicit about the spatial contracts it relies on.
:::
:::

:::

## In 30 seconds

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parent / "src"))

import numpy as np
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "docs" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

roi = ni.spherical_roi(mask, centroid=(4, 4, 2), radius=2)
time_by_voxel = bold.series_roi(roi)

mean_data = np.zeros(mask.shape, dtype=np.float32)
mean_data[tuple(roi.coords.T)] = time_by_voxel.mean(axis=0)
mean_map = ni.NeuroVol.from_array(mean_data, space=mask.space)

time_by_voxel.shape, mean_map.to_nibabel().shape
```

[Read the full tutorial](get-started.qmd)
